comments: TV has a repeated session from Black Axe and an archive Peel session from Blodwyn Pig.

sessions.details: Recorded for Top Gear on 17th June 1974. First Broadcast on John Peel's show on 9th July 1974

track_listing.track: Death On Two Legs (LP - A Night At The Opera) EMI
Stark Raving Love (LP - Bad For Good) Cleveland International
Touch Too Much (LP - Highway To Hell) Atlantic
Rivers (Of The Hidden Funk) (LP - There Goes The Neighborhood) Asylum
Hot River (LP - Nick Mason's Fictitious Sports) Harvest
Echoes And Shadows (LP - Turn Of The Tide) Polydor
The Bells Of Berlin (LP - Firing On All Six) CBS

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. This is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
Right, this very evening we replay a 1974 session out of the archives by Bloodwind Pig.
That's a band that featured some original Jethro Tull members including Mick Abrams.
Also, we repeat our quite recent set by Black Axe.
And we've got some new stuff by Jim Steinman, by Holocaust, by Joe Walsh, by Nick Mason, and also Barclay James Harvest.
You'll be able to hear some classic cuts by ACDC, Jethro Tull, and Lone Star, among others.
Not forgetting our Friday Night Connection, the answer to last week's and a brand new one.
But this week, we start with this.
It's a track from an album called Bad for Good.
It's by Jim Steinman. He's the guy that wrote all the songs on Bad Out of Hell.
Now, Meatloaf was supposed to sing on that album, but according to the story, he decided not to in the end.
and Mr. Steinman laid down the vocals
and they heard Stargraven Love.
